content:
Weekly Review: June 10ГўВҖВ“17, 2026

Г°ВҹВ’Вҡ 12 Days Zyn-Free

Twelve days without Zyn. That's not a small thing, JГғВёrgen. You made a decision on June 5th and you're holding it. Every shift, every stressful moment, every craving you've pushed through has been a win. Keep stacking them.

Г°ВҹВҳВҙ Sleep and Recovery

This is the part we need to talk about honestly. You're averaging 5.9 hours of sleep and a sleep score of 66. For a shift nurse that's not entirely surprising, but it is the single biggest thing holding you back right now. Your HRV is actually solid at 72.5 and your resting heart rate of 44 is excellent, which tells me your body has good underlying fitness. But your training readiness averaging 43.3 is low, and that's the score that matters most for day-to-day performance. The good news is your body battery at wake is averaging 96, which suggests the sleep you are getting is relatively efficient. Still, if there is any week to prioritise even 30 more minutes in bed, it's the next one.

Stress is sitting at 22, which is genuinely low. That's a positive signal, especially five weeks into quitting Zyn when stress responses can spike.

Г°ВҹВҸВғ Training

Two runs, 14.7 km total, and a 5 km walk. Your average pace across both runs was 6:28 per kilometre and your average heart rate was 164 bpm. That puts you right at the top of Zone 3, edging into Zone 4 territory. Given your recovery picture this week, running at that intensity is demanding more than you might realise. The fitness is clearly there given that resting heart rate, but with readiness scores in the low 40s you'd benefit from pulling that effort back slightly on at least one of those runs and keeping it genuinely Zone 2, meaning 125 to 145 bpm. It'll feel almost embarrassingly easy, but that's exactly the point.

You're at 138 out of 150 intensity minutes for the week. Close to your goal. One more easy session would have ticked that over, so that's something to aim for next week.

Г°ВҹВҘВ— Nutrition

Calories came in almost perfectly at 1612 against a target of 1600. That's as clean as it gets. Carbs at 133g were just a touch under the 145g target, and fat at 52g was nearly spot on at 51g. These are minor gaps, nothing to worry about.

Protein is where to focus. You're hitting 110g on average against a target of 150g. That's a 40g daily shortfall, which over the week adds up to around 280g of protein your muscles didn't get. For body composition, recovery, and especially for someone doing physical nursing shifts on top of training, closing that gap matters more than any other nutrition variable right now. Think about where you can add a Greek yoghurt, a protein shake, cottage cheese, or an extra portion of meat or fish each day.

You logged 6 out of 7 days. Good consistency there.

ГўВҡВЎ Looking Ahead

The fundamentals are in decent shape, JГғВёrgen. Your resting metrics are strong, your nutrition is disciplined, and you're training through a demanding schedule. The two things to carry into next week are simple. First, get more sleep wherever the shifts allow it. Second, get that protein up closer to 150g. Everything else will follow those two.

You're doing well. Keep going.
